trial_label: Letrozole in Treating Postmenopausal Women With Stage I, II

rubric.target_patient: A postmenopausal woman with newly diagnosed, resectable invasive breast cancer (clinical stage I-III), HER2-negative by IHC or FISH, who has not yet started any systemic therapy, has archived core biopsy tissue from diagnosis available, and is a surgical candidate for segmental resection or mastectomy. This is a pre-surgical (window-of-opportunity) window trial requiring identification of the patient before her definitive surgery, not after.
rubric.site_burden: Burden must be inferred, as no schedule of assessments was provided. The design implies: identification of newly diagnosed, treatment-naive breast cancer patients prior to their surgical date; a short window (14 days) of oral letrozole dosing (days 7-21) that must be coordinated so as not to delay definitive surgery; retrieval and banking of archived diagnostic core biopsy tissue plus fresh tumor tissue at surgery for Ki67 and biomarker analysis; blood draws for gene expression/RT-PCR; and tight coordination between medical/surgical oncology, pathology, and a tissue-banking or translational research infrastructure capable of handling paraffin-embedded and fresh specimens for a multi-site correlative science protocol. This requires a multidisciplinary breast cancer program with integrated surgical and research pathology support, not just a medical oncology clinic.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a breast cancer specialist (medical oncologist, surgical oncologist, or breast surgeon) capable of managing pre-surgical hormone-sensitive breast cancer patients.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists hematology-oncology, medical oncology, or surgical oncology/breast surgery as the physician's specialty.
criteria.derived_from: Population: postmenopausal women with operable HR-sensitive breast cancer requiring surgical treatment

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have a demonstrated research/clinical interest in breast cancer biology, endocrine therapy, or biomarker-driven trial design, consistent with a translational pre-surgical trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: At least one PubMed-indexed publication authored by the physician on breast cancer, hormone receptor biology, aromatase inhibitors, or Ki67/biomarker response.
criteria.derived_from: Primary outcome: Ki67 index comparison in HR-positive vs HR-negative tumors; rationale around gene expression and biomarker analysis

criteria.requirement: The practice must routinely see newly diagnosed, treatment-naive, surgically resectable breast cancer patients before they proceed to definitive surgery, since enrollment must occur in the pre-surgical window.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician or practice self-attests to managing newly diagnosed, pre-surgical breast cancer patients as a routine part of clinical workflow, or is affiliated with a multidisciplinary breast program per site listing.
criteria.derived_from: Design: pre-surgical letrozole trial requiring identification prior to segmental resection/mastectomy; inclusion of no prior chemotherapy for this primary breast cancer

criteria.requirement: The practice must see a sufficient volume of postmenopausal, HER2-negative, stage I-III breast cancer patients to support meaningful accrual.
criteria.satisfies_if: Medicare data shows billing for breast cancer diagnosis/treatment codes (e.g., E&M plus chemotherapy administration or surgical pathology) at a volume consistent with a dedicated breast oncology practice, understood as a floor on true volume.
criteria.derived_from: Eligibility: postmenopausal, HER2-negative (0/+1 IHC or non-amplified FISH), clinical stage I-III invasive breast cancer

criteria.requirement: The site must have infrastructure to retrieve archived paraffin-embedded diagnostic core biopsy material and to collect/process fresh surgical tissue and blood for translational biomarker (Ki67, gene expression, RT-PCR) analysis.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site self-attests to having an on-site or affiliated tissue banking/pathology research infrastructure capable of retrieving archived paraffin blocks and processing fresh surgical specimens for correlative science.
criteria.derived_from: Requirement for core biopsies from diagnosis to be available; tissue collection and biomarker analysis of surgical specimens; blood collection for gene expression/RT-PCR

criteria.requirement: The site must have integrated access to breast surgical services (segmental resection or mastectomy) so that the pre-surgical dosing window can be coordinated without delaying definitive surgery.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site self-attests to having in-house or closely coordinated breast surgical oncology services able to schedule surgery within the trial's dosing window (day 7-21 post-enrollment).
criteria.derived_from: Interventions: letrozole days 7-21 followed by biopsy/lumpectomy/mastectomy; inclusion criterion of planning segmental resection or total mastectomy

criteria.requirement: The site should function within an academic cancer center or cooperative group-style environment capable of supporting a SPORE-affiliated, multi-institution translational study with only 5 participating sites.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site is an academic cancer center, NCI-designated cancer center, or is listed among the trial's 5 participating sites per ClinicalTrials.gov record.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: Vanderbilt-Ingram Cancer Center (SPORE); Sites: Emory, Vanderbilt-Ingram (x2), Allegheny Cancer Center, Surgical Associates Inc.

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have prior experience serving as a site investigator on interventional oncology trials, demonstrating capacity for protocol compliance and GCP conduct.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov lists the physician as an investigator (PI or sub-I) on at least one prior or concurrent interventional oncology trial.
criteria.derived_from: Trial design: EARLY_PHASE1 interventional pre-surgical window trial requiring protocol-driven enrollment timing

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have some history of receiving industry research payments or grants indicative of active clinical trial participation, relevant to running an aromatase-inhibitor-based translational study.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments database shows research-category payments (not general/consulting) to the physician from a pharmaceutical sponsor, at any point in their record.
criteria.derived_from: Intervention: letrozole (aromatase inhibitor) administered per protocol; trial requires sponsor-coordinated drug supply and monitoring

criteria.requirement: The investigator or site should have a track record of participating in cooperative/SPORE-sponsored translational breast cancer research rather than only sponsor-initiated commercial trials.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov shows the physician or site as a participant in at least one other academically-sponsored (non-industry, e.g. NCI, SPORE, cooperative group) breast cancer trial.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor type: Vanderbilt-Ingram Cancer Center (OTHER, SPORE-designated translational study)

excluded_screening: ECOG performance status 0-1 — a per-patient functional assessment at enrollment, not a marker of physician or site capability.
Bilirubin ≤1.5x ULN — a per-patient lab eligibility threshold checked at screening, irrelevant to site fitness.
Creatinine ≤1.5x ULN — a per-patient renal function lab threshold checked at screening, irrelevant to site fitness.
No dementia/altered mental status precluding informed consent — an individual patient capacity assessment, not a site-level criterion.
No prior resection of stomach or small bowel — an individual patient anatomic/absorption exclusion, not indicative of physician expertise or site capacity.
No other malignancy within past 5 years (with exceptions) — a per-patient oncologic history exclusion applied at screening.
No prior chemotherapy for this primary breast cancer — a per-patient treatment history check at enrollment, though it does inform the population description already captured elsewhere.
No evidence of distant metastatic disease — a per-patient staging/imaging confirmation at screening, not a site capability marker.
